SyedJaveed

Cybersecurity Engineer — Privileged Access Management · 5+ Years Experience

Freelancer Ready to Hire

Security engineer specializing in Privileged Access Management and identity security, with hands-on expertise across CyberArk and HashiCorp Vault. I design and operate solutions that secure privileged credentials, enforce least-privilege access, and automate secrets management across hybrid and cloud environments.

$ about --profile

How I approach the work

My work centers on reducing attack surface and eliminating credential sprawl — implementing PAM controls, integrating IAM workflows, and building secure, scalable secrets architectures that keep pace with modern DevOps pipelines. I bridge the gap between security rigor and operational efficiency, ensuring sensitive access is protected without slowing teams down.

CORE FOCUS
Privileged Access Management (CyberArk) Secrets Management (HashiCorp Vault) Identity & Access Management Zero-Trust Access DevSecOps Integration

$ services --list

Privileged access, engineered end to end

Two platforms, one discipline: nothing stays privileged longer than it needs to.

CyberArk

Full-lifecycle PAM — vault architecture, credential rotation, session isolation and recording, and zero standing privilege access.

  • Vault & CPM design and hardening
  • PVWA / PSM & PSMP deployment
  • Endpoint Privilege Manager (EPM)
  • SIA — Zero Standing Privilege access
  • Conjur secrets for DevOps pipelines
Discuss a CyberArk engagement

HashiCorp

Secrets management and dynamic credentials for cloud-native and DevOps environments, built around HashiCorp Vault.

  • Vault policy & auth method design
  • Dynamic database & cloud secrets
  • PKI & certificate automation
  • Kubernetes secret injection
  • Terraform-driven provisioning
Discuss a Vault engagement
$ domains --scope

Where I work

Four connected disciplines, one goal — the right identity, the right access, for exactly as long as it's needed.

01

Cyber Security

Threat-aware architecture and hardening across identity, infrastructure, and endpoints.

02

IAM

Identity governance, access lifecycle, and least-privilege enforcement across the workforce.

03

PAM

Vaulting, session control, and credential rotation for privileged human and non-human identities.

04

Secrets Management

Dynamic, short-lived secrets for applications, pipelines, and cloud workloads.

$ projects --list

Selected Projects

A sample of PAM, IAM, and secrets management engagements delivered across enterprise environments. Details editable — placeholders for your specific engagements.

Enterprise CyberArk Vault Rollout

End-to-end Vault, CPM, and PVWA/PSM deployment onboarding thousands of privileged accounts across a global estate.

CyberArkPVWAPSM

Zero Standing Privilege Migration

Moved static admin access to an ephemeral, just-in-time access model, closing standing-privilege exposure across Windows and Linux estates.

SIA / DPAZSPWindows & Linux

DevOps Secrets Automation

Integrated Conjur and HashiCorp Vault into CI/CD pipelines to eliminate hardcoded credentials from application code and build systems.

ConjurHashiCorp VaultCI/CD

IAM Access Governance Program

Built access certification workflows and least-privilege review cycles across a multi-thousand-user directory.

IAMAccess Reviews

Endpoint Privilege Manager Rollout

Deployed EPM policy sets fleet-wide to remove standing local admin rights from end-user endpoints.

EPMEndpoint Security

PKI & Certificate Automation

Automated short-lived certificate issuance for internal services using Vault-backed PKI, replacing manual renewal processes.

PKIVault
